# Plan: Validation Engine Enhancement

## 1. Overview

Enhance the Wave Client validation engine with production-grade JSON path evaluation and JSON schema validation, unify the two divergent rule editor UIs into a single shared component, and improve the overall validation UX with tooltips, placeholder examples, and real-time schema validation feedback. This work targets `packages/shared` (engine) and `packages/core` (UI), keeping all changes platform-agnostic.

## 2. High-Level Features

| FEAT-ID | Feature | Spec File | Status |
|---------|---------|-----------|--------|
| FEAT-001 | Validation Engine Enhancement — Replace naive JSON path with `jsonpath-plus`, replace stub JSON schema validator with `ajv`, add `validateJsonSchemaString` helper for UI pre-validation | [`features/feature_validation_engine_enhancement.md`](.spec-lite/features/feature_validation_engine_enhancement.md) | [x] Complete |
| FEAT-002 | Unified Validation Rule Editor — Extract common `ValidationRuleEditor` component from `ValidationWizard` and `RuleEditorDialog`, ensure consistent styling across `ValidationStoreGrid` and `RequestValidation` | [`features/feature_unified_rule_editor.md`](.spec-lite/features/feature_unified_rule_editor.md) | [x] Complete |
| FEAT-003 | UX Polish — Tooltips, Placeholders & Schema Validation — Add category/operator tooltips, rich placeholder examples, body-content tooltip, JSON schema validity feedback in editor | [`features/feature_validation_ux_polish.md`](.spec-lite/features/feature_validation_ux_polish.md) | [x] Complete |

> **Note**: The `Spec File` column is populated by the **Feature sub-agent** when it creates each feature spec. The `Status` column is owned exclusively by the **Implement sub-agent** — it marks `[/] In progress` when implementation begins and `[x] Complete` when all tasks are verified.

## 3. Tech Stack Additions

> The canonical tech stack is defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Tech Stack.

| Component | Technology | Justification |
|-----------|-----------|---------------|
| JSON Path evaluation | `jsonpath-plus` (MIT, latest) | Replaces the naive dot-notation/array-access JSON path parser in `validationEngine.ts`. Supports full JSONPath spec including recursive descent (`..`), wildcards (`*`), filters (`[?()]`), unions, and slicing. Well-maintained with 2M+ weekly npm downloads. |
| JSON Schema validation | `ajv` (MIT, latest) | Replaces the stub `validateJsonSchema` function that only checked JSON parse-ability. Battle-tested JSON Schema validator supporting draft-04/06/07/2019-09/2020-12. Used by ESLint, webpack, and 50M+ weekly downloads. |

**Package placement:**
- `ajv` → `packages/shared` (used by the validation engine at runtime)
- `jsonpath-plus` → `packages/shared` (used by the validation engine at runtime)
- A `validateJsonSchemaString(schemaString: string)` utility will be exported from `packages/shared` for UI-side pre-validation of user-provided schemas

## 4. Data Model (High-Level)

No data model changes required. The existing validation types (`ValidationRule`, `BodyValidationRule`, `GlobalValidationRule`, etc. in `packages/core/src/types/validation.ts`) already define all necessary fields for JSON path (`jsonPath` field) and JSON schema (`value` field with `json_schema_matches` operator). The `BodyOperator` type already includes `json_path_exists`, `json_path_equals`, `json_path_contains`, and `json_schema_matches`.

## 5. Interface Design

### Engine API Changes (packages/shared)

**Modified functions (internal):**

| Function | Change |
|----------|--------|
| `evaluateJsonPath(body, jsonPath)` | Rewrite to use `jsonpath-plus` `JSONPath()`. Returns `{ value: any; found: boolean; error?: string }` (same signature). Now supports full JSONPath spec (recursive descent, wildcards, filters, etc.). |
| `validateJsonSchema(body, schema)` | Rewrite to use `ajv.compile(schema).validate(data)`. Returns `{ valid: boolean; error?: string }` (same signature). Now performs real JSON Schema validation. |

**New exports:**

| Function | Signature | Purpose |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| `validateJsonSchemaString` | `(schemaString: string) => { valid: boolean; errors?: string[] }` | UI-side helper to validate that a user-provided JSON string is a valid JSON Schema. Parses the string, compiles with ajv, and returns validation errors if the schema itself is malformed. Exported from `packages/shared`. |

### UI Component Changes (packages/core)

**New component:**

| Component | Location | Purpose |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| `ValidationRuleEditor` | `packages/core/src/components/common/ValidationRuleEditor.tsx` | Unified rule editor form extracted from `ValidationWizard` and `RuleEditorDialog`. Handles all categories (status, header, body, time) with consistent styling, tooltips, and placeholder examples. Used by both `ValidationStoreGrid` (via dialog) and `RequestValidation` (via dialog). |

**Modified components:**

| Component | Change |
|-----------|--------|
| `ValidationWizard` | Refactor to delegate form rendering to `ValidationRuleEditor`. Keep the wizard's `onSave`/`onCancel` API and name validation logic. Becomes a thin wrapper. |
| `RuleEditorDialog` (in `RequestValidation.tsx`) | Refactor to use `ValidationRuleEditor` inside its dialog shell. Remove duplicated form fields. |
| `RequestValidation` | Minor: ensure table column widths and styling match `ValidationStoreGrid` patterns. |

### Tooltip & Placeholder Enhancements

| Location | Enhancement |
|----------|-------------|
| Body category | Tooltip: "Body validation only supports text-based response content (JSON, XML, HTML, plain text). Binary responses are not supported." |
| JSON Path field | Placeholder: `e.g., $.data.users[0].name` or `$.items[*].id`. Tooltip: "JSONPath expression to navigate the JSON response body. Uses jsonpath-plus syntax." |
| JSON Schema field | Placeholder: multiline example schema. Tooltip: "Provide a valid JSON Schema (draft-04 through 2020-12). The response body will be validated against this schema." |
| JSON Schema editor | Real-time validation indicator showing whether the entered schema is valid JSON Schema, with error messages. |
| Status value | Placeholder: `e.g., 200` |
| Header name | Placeholder: `e.g., Content-Type, Authorization, X-Request-Id` |
| Time value | Placeholder: `e.g., 1000` with label suffix "(ms)" |
| Regex value | Placeholder: `e.g., ^[0-9a-f]{8}-...` |

## 6. Security Considerations

No plan-specific concerns — see memory. All inputs (JSON path expressions, JSON schemas, expected values) are already evaluated in a sandboxed context within the validation engine. `ajv` does not execute arbitrary code when validating schemas. `jsonpath-plus` filter expressions could theoretically execute code — we should disable `eval` mode and use `safe` mode (default in jsonpath-plus v10+).

**Specific mitigation:** When initializing jsonpath-plus, ensure `preventEval: true` is set to prevent script injection through crafted JSONPath filter expressions.

## 7. Architecture & Design (Plan-Specific)

### Plan-Specific Decisions

- **Separate concerns for jsonpath-plus and ajv**: Keep them as independent utilities rather than combining via ajv custom keywords. JSON path evaluation and JSON schema validation serve different operators (`json_path_*` vs `json_schema_matches`) and combining them adds unnecessary coupling. This is simpler, easier to test, and matches the existing operator-based dispatch pattern.
- **Schema validation helper in packages/shared**: The `validateJsonSchemaString` helper lives in `packages/shared` rather than `packages/core` because ajv is a shared dependency and the helper has no UI concerns. Core components import it for UI feedback.
- **Unified editor as a form component, not a dialog**: `ValidationRuleEditor` is a pure form component (no dialog wrapper). Both `ValidationWizard` and `RuleEditorDialog` wrap it in their respective dialog shells. This maximizes reuse without forcing a specific dialog implementation.
- **Consistent styling via the ValidationWizard pattern**: The `ValidationStoreGrid` styling (which uses `ValidationWizard` with `Label`, `Input`, `Select`, `Switch`) is the canonical style. `RuleEditorDialog` will adopt the same pattern by switching to `ValidationRuleEditor`.

## 8. Coding Standards (Plan-Specific Overrides)

No overrides — see memory.

## 9. Testing Strategy (Plan-Specific)

### FEAT-001: Validation Engine Tests

**Location:** `packages/shared/src/test/utils/validationEngine.test.ts` (extend existing)

| Test Area | Scenarios |
|-----------|-----------|
| `evaluateJsonPath` with jsonpath-plus | Recursive descent (`$..id`), wildcards (`$.items[*].name`), filters (`$.items[?(@.price > 10)]`), array slicing (`$.items[0:3]`), nested paths, non-existent paths, malformed expressions, non-JSON body |
| `validateJsonSchema` with ajv | Valid schema + matching body, valid schema + non-matching body (with error details), invalid JSON body, complex schemas (nested objects, arrays, refs, patterns, required fields) |
| `validateJsonSchemaString` (new) | Valid schema string → `{ valid: true }`, invalid JSON string → errors, valid JSON but invalid schema → errors, empty string → errors |
| Security | JSONPath with `preventEval` — ensure filter expressions don't execute arbitrary code |

### FEAT-002 & FEAT-003: UI Tests

**Location:** `packages/core/src/test/components/`

| Test Area | Scenarios |
|-----------|-----------|
| `ValidationRuleEditor` | Renders all fields for each category, category change resets fields, operator change shows/hides conditional fields, JSON schema validation indicator works, tooltips render |
| `ValidationStoreGrid` integration | Add/edit flows use the unified editor |
| `RequestValidation` integration | Add/edit inline rule flows use the unified editor |

## 10. Logging Strategy (Plan-Specific)

No additions — see memory. Validation engine errors are already surfaced via `ValidationRuleResult.error` and `message` fields.
